biography: Matti entered the competitive sphere of the Hector Trophée on two occasions, marking his presence in this distinguished pairs competition. His participation, though limited, demonstrated an engagement with the tournament's unique multi-day format, which rigorously tests both individual skill and strategic partnership.

Across these two demanding outings, Matti engaged resolutely with the multi-faceted competitive landscape of the Hector Trophée. Despite his concerted efforts, the ultimate prizes — the coveted Hector Trophée for the victorious pair and the prestigious Victor Trophy for the leading individual performer — did not come to fruition under his name, underscoring the exacting standards of the event.

With his involvement in Hector events now concluded, Matti is considered to have stepped away from active competition. Consequently, he will not be among the distinguished field for the upcoming Hector Trophée 2026. His brief but resolute participation remains a part of the tournament's rich history, illustrating the dedication required to contend at this level.
